name: Hindafjall — The Wall Falls
tagline: Erik seizes Sepermeru as the Cursed Wall crumbles and the Exiles are unleashed once more.
lore: Long months spent studying, observing, planning. Months of labor, of death, of love, and sweat. The Witch Queen, Hathor Ka, ordered the Exiles to destroy the final piece of the puzzle — a weakened obelisk of the Cursed Wall, created eons past by the Giant Kings. The attack succeeded despite many wounded and dead. The Exiles found the obelisk's foundation, fought and defeated the eldritch nightmares summoned to defend it, and bled until the Wall, destabilised, fell. But not before its power spun out of control — only the Witch Queen's own intervention prevented the death of everyone in the Exiled Lands. What followed came quickly. Erik the Red, always spying for a moment of weakness, took advantage while Hathor Ka lay exhausted and bedridden. Sepermeru was invaded by the Vanir horde, the puppet Sultan deposed, a new order installed. The Exiles — many strong warriors or sorcerous adepts — were subdued and imprisoned as a precaution, their powers left to rot in cells and special bindings. Then, five months after the Wall fell and Erik's rule was secure, the order came: release the Exiles, dress them, arm them. Their new tasks await. What will you do, Exile, until Erik's next call to arms? Wander the wilds for fortune? Remain in Hindafjall's Inn, content to survive? Bend to those with stronger will, a slave? Or raise an army to be feared and respected? The story is yours to write...

sections.title: Hindafjall
sections.body: A somewhat hidden fortress town, Hindafjall serves as this season's hub. Led by Erik the Red and his Vanir, Exiles are kept under the faction's thumb — and Erik's whims.

sections.title: The Inn
sections.body: The Inn makes a return! You may request a private room to live in and decorate for the rest of the season. Primarily tailored to solo players who don't want to build, but may be available to clan players if there's space. Solo players will mechanically join the hub faction to decorate. Security is provided by RPR locks. Due to technical limitations we can't offer hub clan access without supervision, so self-decorating is restricted to:
list: Requesting a pre-decorated room
Waiting until an admin is there to supervise you

sections.title: Hindafjall and Housing Plots
sections.body: Plots are scattered around the hub. Build freely up to a sprawl of about 8x8 foundations. Don't build higher than the main hub buildings, keep it inside the city limits, and build lag-friendly — we'll come by and check. Build small and immersive. To claim a plot, open a ticket.

sections.title: In Character Cost of Living
sections.body: Real estate is expensive, even in Exile. If your character builds at Hindafjall or rooms in The Inn, rent applies. How much and how often is up to you — but assume your character always pays up. There is no mechanical enforcement. Purely roleplay: a hook to use, or ignore.

sections.title: The Crafting Basement
sections.body: To let hub dwellers craft everything they need, a private crafting hall is available in addition to the public hub benches. Access it through the Craft Basement warp with a special role. Plenty of stations are available, and you're encouraged to capture thralls for the workbenches. Play nice: don't steal, don't clutter. Cooperate out of character to get what you need — so you can get at each other's throats in character!

sections.title: The Hub and Conflict
sections.body: The 'no fighting at the hub' rule is reduced. PvP near Hindafjall and the Inn is now allowed. Keep these areas PvP-free — and don't hide or flee into them. Repeated violent behavior might draw the ire of a certain Sultan:
list: The Merchant and Crafting Area
The Tavern parts of the Inn (not corridors, not rooms)
All quest-relevant buildings (those with named NPCs)

sections.title: The Hub and Raids
sections.body: Raids are possible at the hub, but you may not attack the hub faction itself. You can raid an Inn room (breaking the door) or a plot. Explosives are banned in the Inn but allowed when raiding a player build in the hub. T2/T3 raids against a hub plot must be done without trebuchets; all other explosives are allowed.

sections.title: Earning Crowns
sections.body: Crowns are the main currency. You might ask — how do I gain these ominous crowns? The answer is as simple as it is plentiful:
list: Your starting funds of fifty silver crowns. Spend them wisely!
Roleplay is the most fun way to earn coin. Offer services or goods to other players.
Daily Quests are available! NPCs in Hindafjall may have reoccurring tasks for you.
Champions in the Savage Wilds, accessible through the little war camp, provide extra coin.
Hidden treasures and sunken chests are scattered throughout Exile, where long lost savages buried their coin.

sections.title: Player Market
sections.body: Built on the shores of the northern lake. Set down a merchant and sell wares yourself or via NPC. Use the existing central stalls, or build your own small market building around it. Keep it as small as possible — it counts against your build limits.

sections.title: NPC Announcements
sections.body: Three new ways to announce your presence and seek Roleplay. Interacting with these NPCs sends a message in Global Chat for everyone to see:
list: The Stockades: Imprison yourself in Sepermeru's stocks, leaving your fate to chance!
The Slave Auction: Announce your intention to sell a slave in Hindafjall, leading to an impromptu auction!
The Arena: Call fellow warriors to spar with you in Hindafjall's Arena!

sections.title: Available Quests
sections.body: Several quests are available again, with more to come. IMPORTANT: do not cancel quest dialogues with the X button — it won't break the quest, but it may not forward the quest status properly.
list: One larger quest line introducing the people running Hindafjall, with a nice reward — find the start yourself!
Erik the Red offers his quests again
Magda the Milk Maid has moved into Sepermeru with ancient knowledge
A new line of quests requiring you to delve into the depths below the Wilds again
Master Thief Lucy has some brilliant ideas again
A fortune teller has found her way to Hindafjall
Random quests, encounterable in the wild

sections.title: Roleplay Redux Locks
sections.body: RPR locks secure your doors and containers. Make a Keymaker's Toolbox, place it, then craft a key — each key is unique and named. Track which key fits which door (clan MOTD or RPR notes). Use keys to lock doors and containers; duplicate keys to share. There's a good in-game tooltip — check it out.

sections.title: Other Information
list: Delvin Melvin found strange recipes in Vaults — furniture and smaller crafting stations. Check them out.
TW recipe journals can now be found in Hindafjall — check the bookshelves.
There are fishtraps and beehives at the hub now — feel free to use them.
Plenty of 'portals' (ICly tunnels) form an interconnected network, marked by swirly map icons.

sections.title: World Events
sections.body: World Events and Quests return. Exiles may encounter Points of Interest while exploring, both in the Exiled Lands and the Isle of Women. Most take the form of repeatable Quests — the first to complete a line gets a special reward. POIs lead to new places, tombs with traps and riddles, treasures, or cultists summoning eldritch Lords. Designed to be solved without admin intervention. Unique loot is designed to promote conflict — once found, rumours spread, and anyone might steal, lie, cheat, or go to war for it. Keep an eye on your quest log (Shift+U) and DM tickets!

sections.title: Goat Tokens
sections.body: Plaque Goat Tokens are the IC stipend players receive as payment for their service as Erik's Soldiers. They are paid out during events, found in ancient ruins, and given as a one-time reward (once per player) for completing World Event quests. They can be exchanged for unique rewards in Hindafjall — with the Quartermaster, the Sorceress, and more.

sections.title: The Deep Roads
sections.body: The Deep Roads are tunnels for safer travel, overhauled this season. The map is divided into sectors, each with one deep road to Hindafjall. Erik the Red grants Deep Roads permits — exchanging rare relics for the manpower to dig two additional tunnels per sector. Permits can be traded, stolen, or raided, and guarantee a tunnel for up to three weeks before going up for grabs again, unless the owning clan can keep it safe.

sections.title: Thanes (Viceroys)
sections.body: Viceroyalties return, revamped for the new 'king' of the Exiled Lands, Erik the Red. The Exiled Lands are divided into three great regions, each hiding the foundations of a fort. The first player to find it, buy a Viceroy emblem from the Hub, and open a ticket to claim gains ownership of the whole Region as Erik's Governor. Your will isn't admin-enforced — any rules you make must be enforced by you. The mechanic exists to facilitate conflict and battle. Any Region Fortress can be attacked and conquered with a Tier 2 raid at any time, with 24 hours of warning; once attacked, win or lose, it has a 7-day cooldown to rebuild. Erik won't allow the death of his Exiles, but other consequences follow normal RP tiers — and the losing side may not RP defeat unrealistically or resist winners constantly. Outside conquest raids, these forts are like any player build: raidable for T1/T2/T3 (if the viceroyalty stays in place), robbable, and usable for RP and decoration. Players in Viceroy Clans may not participate in other territories' Viceroy Raids, in defense or offense.

sections.title: Thane Contests
sections.body: Erik wishes to test his new Thanes to ensure their ability to rule. When an Exile attempts to claim a position, others have 7 days to collect an Emblem, then participate in a contest to decide the new Thane — decided randomly by Erik, be it wits, horse racing, or plain old fighting. Why bother? Erik pays no stipend, but wishes to observe his Thanes in pitched battle. In the Nexus region — the center of the new Borderlands — stands a fortress. Whoever occupies it and holds it for 60 minutes receives a rich reward from a pool of materials, rare items, weapons, and more. The catch: the moment you trigger the script, the whole Exiled Lands is announced that someone is going for the reward! Only a Viceroy can trigger it, but the reward goes to the last man standing after 60 minutes. These raids trigger every 14 days, Friday evening, starting 90 minutes after evening reset until two hours after at most. Teams of 8 players maximum.
